subject: Project Management
title: Batch Size and Flow
description: A fast breakdown of Batch Size and Flow for builders.

hook.text: Would you rather fold 100 letters, stuff 100 envelopes, then stamp 100 — or do them one at a time?

buildup.text: Batching feels efficient but creates huge queues between steps. Small batches flow faster.

discovery.text: In software, small batches mean smaller releases, faster feedback, and fewer bugs.

twist.text: Reducing batch size feels riskier. But each small batch is easier to test, review, and roll back.

climax.text: Small batches are Kanban's practical secret: less work in flight means less risk everywhere.

punchline.text: Ship less at once. Ship more overall.

quiz.question: Why do smaller batches improve flow?
options: They reduce queues between steps and speed up feedback
They eliminate the need for testing
